content.text:
<user_query>
Review humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh and humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh against requested items: SSH alias resolution, experiment propagation, seed handling, queue/site duplication, parser and GPU alias behavior, confirmation/reporting semantics. Return a concise list of highest-severity risks with line-level references and any mismatches against requested intent.
</user_query>

I found several high-impact mismatches against the requested checks.

1. [Critical] Confirmation/reporting semantics are effectively bypassed. `fuyao_deploy.sh:364-367` hard-sets `REPLY=y` and never actually reads user input; the “Please verify…” prompt is never interactive unless `-y` is supplied, so accidental submissions are likely when confirmation was expected.
2. [High] Seed handling is implicitly mandatory and can block launches unintentionally. `fuyao_deploy.sh:13` sets `override_seed=true` by default, and `fuyao_deploy.sh:320-324` requires seed count to match `num_jobs`, so running without `-s` will fail even for single-job runs. This is a behavior mismatch if defaults should allow implicit/random or config-driven seeds.
3. [High] Experiment propagation is incomplete for training metadata. `fuyao_deploy.sh:153-154` sends `--experiment` to the `fuyao deploy` command, but `fuyao_train.sh` does not parse/forward an experiment argument, and run naming is built from only task+label at `fuyao_train.sh:146-149` and `fuyao_train.sh:121-123`, so experiment-level organization/reporting inside training is not preserved.
4. [Medium] Queue/site arguments can be duplicated/contradicted by user-supplied `fuyao_args`. `fuyao_deploy.sh:147-155` unconditionally injects `--site` and `--queue`, while `fuyao_deploy.sh:219-222` passes raw `fuyao_args` through; if users include `--site/--queue` there, the resulting command may have conflicting duplicates.
5. [Medium] SSH alias resolution is not implemented in the wrapper layer. The deploy parser/flow (`fuyao_deploy.sh:185-242`) has no SSH/host alias option or resolution path, so alias-based target selection is entirely delegated to external environment without explicit, validated handling in these scripts.

If you want, I can propose a minimal patch set to fix items 1–4 in a safe, low-diff way.
